name: Sierra de Alcubierre – circular from Tardienta
name_original: Sierra de Alcubierre – circular desde Tardienta
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Tardienta are situated within the unique semi-desert landscape of Los Monegros in the Hoya de Huesca region. This area features arid plains and low mountains, offering varied terrain for road cyclists. The Sierra de Alcubierre, a range of low mountains, provides opportunities for ascents and descents, while other sections feature mostly well-paved, flatter surfaces. The region's distinctive natural features make it suitable for a range of road cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Tardienta The most popular road cycling route is Grañén, Tardienta, Almudévar, a 41.4 miles (66.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 44 minutes to complete. This route connects several towns across the plains of the Hoya de Huesca. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sierra de Alcubierre – circular from Tardienta, a moderate 65.8 miles (105.9 km) path. This route navigates the low mountains of the Sierra de Alcubierre, offering varied elevation profiles. Local road cyclists also love the Monegros Canal Route, a 78.0 miles (125.6 km) trail leading through the Monegros landscape, often completed in about 4 hours 53 minutes. Road cycling around Tardienta is defined by arid plains, the low mountains of Sierra de Alcubierre, and routes following the Monegros Canal. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels. The routes in Tardienta are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Tardienta's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Tardienta?
A: There are 7 road cycling routes available around Tardienta, offering a variety of experiences through the unique landscape of Los Monegros and the Sierra de Alcubierre.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling near Tardienta?
A: The terrain around Tardienta is diverse, characterized by the semi-desert environment of Los Monegros. You'll find arid plains, which offer relatively flat sections, as well as the low mountains of the Sierra de Alcubierre, providing opportunities for more challenging ascents and descents. Most routes feature well-paved surfaces.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of road cycling routes in Tardienta?
A: The road cycling routes in Tardienta cater to moderate to difficult ability levels. There are 6 moderate routes and 1 difficult route, ensuring options for cyclists with good fitness looking for varied elevation profiles.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see along road cycling routes in Tardienta?
A: While cycling, you can explore several historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2739043" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orwell Trenches&lt;/a&gt; in the Sierra de Alcubierre offer insight into the Spanish Civil War. You might also pass through &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6362271" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almudévar&lt;/a&gt;, a local settlement, or see the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5771132" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of Magallón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any natural features to look out for while cycling in the region?
A: Yes, the region is known for its unique semi-desert landscape of Los Monegros, with arid plains and the low mountains of the Sierra de Alcubierre. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247249" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdabra Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, a notable water feature in this arid environment, or cycle alongside the Monegros Canal.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes from Tardienta?
A: Yes, a prominent circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e926173190/sierra-de-alcubierre-circular-from-tardienta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Alcubierre – circular from Tardienta&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate 105.9 km path navigates the low mountains, offering varied elevation profiles and a comprehensive tour of the area.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Tardienta?
A: Given the semi-desert climate, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for road cycling in Tardienta, offering milder temperatures compared to the intense heat of summer. Winter can also be suitable, though temperatures will be cooler.

Q: Is there parking available for cyclists in Tardienta?
A: While specific designated cyclist parking isn't detailed, Tardienta, as a village, typically offers general parking options. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for the most convenient places to park your vehicle.

Q: Can I reach Tardienta by public transport with my bike?
A: Information on public transport specifically accommodating bikes to Tardienta is not readily available. It's recommended to check with local bus or train operators in the Huesca province for their bike carriage policies before planning your trip.

Q: Are there cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the routes?
A: While the routes themselves traverse a semi-desert landscape, many pass through or near towns like Grañén, Tardienta, and Almudévar. These settlements are likely to offer cafes, bars, or small shops where you can stop for refreshments and refuel during your ride.

Q: Are there accommodations suitable for cyclists in Tardienta?
A: Tardienta and nearby towns offer various accommodation options, from guesthouses to small hotels. While not all may be specifically cyclist-focused, many can accommodate bikes. It's best to inquire directly with establishments about secure bike storage when booking.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Tardienta?
A: The road cycling routes around Tardienta are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. Cyclists often appreciate the unique semi-desert landscape and the well-paved surfaces that make for an enjoyable ride.
tips: Its old quarter stands on a gentle tozal, where the church and the remains of an old castle predominate over the modern part of the town.

The English writer and journalist George Orwell participated as a volunteer of the Republic in the Monegros front. Following his story, trenches, bunkers and other elements that remained in the arid lands of the Aragonese steppe have been recovered. From here you can access a hill with a trench where the writer was, from which you can see the towers of Pilar de Zaragoza.
Nice views of the Río Gallego from the bridge on the Huesca highway.
